name: CLI
summary: Entry point and orchestrator; bin/claude-pilot.js interactive menu and lifecycle management

name: Session
summary: tmux-based session lifecycle: create, watch, auto-resume, remove offline

name: Web
summary: Optional HTTP dashboard and JSON API for remote session monitoring

name: Core
summary: Shared utilities: config persistence (~/.claude-pilot/config.json) and Telegram notifications

name: Launch Session
summary: Spawn a new Claude Code session in a named tmux window

name: List Sessions
summary: Display active/offline sessions in interactive readline menu

name: Manage Session Lifecycle
summary: Orchestrate start/stop/remove sessions; graceful exit via 'exit' command or SIGTERM; Ctrl+C is safe (shows hint only); watch sorted by status, j/k navigation for >9 sessions

name: Create Session
summary: Create tmux window, send claude command, register in config, start Watcher

name: Auto-Resume Session
summary: Restart Claude in existing tmux window on unexpected exit

name: Watch Process
summary: Poll tmux pane for exit or stale output; parse usage limits with reset times (including timezone); prevent retry spam when limit persists; fire callbacks

name: Remove Offline Sessions
summary: Remove dead sessions from config and kill tmux windows

name: Serve Dashboard
summary: Serve embedded HTML dashboard with session status and version info; writes timestamped debug log to ~/.claude/pilot-web-debug.log with per-request timing and slow broadcast detection

name: Session API
summary: GET /api/sessions and POST /api/sessions/:name/action endpoints

name: Git Diff & Commit Panel
summary: GET git/status, GET git/diff, POST git/commit — exposes workspace changes and allows committing from the dashboard

name: Open Finder
summary: POST open-finder — runs 'open <cwd>' to open the session directory in macOS Finder

name: Token Authentication
summary: Single static token auth via ?token= query param or Bearer header

name: Load Config
summary: Read ~/.claude-pilot/config.json; return defaults if missing

name: Save Config
summary: Persist in-memory config to ~/.claude-pilot/config.json

name: Send Notification
summary: Send Telegram message via curl on session events; silent if unconfigured

name: Launch Flow
summary: Startup to supervised session: config load → setup → web start → menu → create session → watch

name: Watch and Resume Flow
summary: Poll → detect exit → resume → notify

name: Web Request Flow
summary: Token check → route → read config → response; each request logged with timing; broadcast cycles block event loop — root cause of API stalls after SSE connects

name: Tmux Session Model
summary: Claude sessions = tmux windows inside one tmux session; window name = session name

name: Auto-Resume Concept
summary: Watcher re-sends claude command on unexpected exit; fails silently if tmux window was killed

name: Web Token Auth Concept
summary: Static token, no rotation, designed for trusted-network use only

name: Config Persistence Concept
summary: Single JSON file, no cache singleton, no database; concurrent write risk exists

name: Telegram Notification Concept
summary: curl-based, opt-in, fail-silent; no SDK dependency

name: Setup Preferences Concept
summary: First-run interactive wizard; skipped when setupDone=true in config

name: Web Terminal UI Concept
summary: React SPA in lib/ui.html renders ANSI output, handles click-to-focus, and guards text selection before stealing input focus

name: Static Web Token — No Rotation
summary: Web auth token never rotates; if config file is world-readable, token is permanently exposed

name: Config Concurrent Write Risk
summary: No locking on config.json writes; concurrent mutations from CLI and web server could race

name: Resume Fails When tmux Window Killed
summary: Auto-resume silently fails if the tmux window (not just the process) was killed; no user feedback
